item.title: Deploy Hugo on your Homserver via GitHub Actions
item.description:
Prerequisites

You need to have:

  • A GitHub account
  • A Hugo website in a private repository, pushed to GitHub
  • A Linux homeserver with Caddy Reverse Proxy running.
  • A domain (or duckdns)

If not, then this guide is not the perfect match for you (yet). However, you can get a GitHub account and set it up with this guide Git & GitHub Essentials . Another of my blogs covers How to create a blog with Hugo . Getting your own Linux homeserver is also doable, e.g. with a Raspi. Come back to this afterwards.

item.title: Install Custom Applications on Linux
item.description:
Overview

When using Linux machines where you lack root privileges (sudo), you can still install applications locally. This guide covers how to set up standalone binaries (like VS Code, Obsidian, or custom tools) and integrate them into your desktop environment’s app launcher using .desktop files.

Step-by-Step Installation

1. Download and Prepare

Download the executable or compressed archive (.tar.gz, .zip, .AppImage) for your application. If it’s a folder, move it to a dedicated local bin directory.

item.title: Git & GitHub
item.description:
“Git is a distributed version control software system that is capable of managing versions of source code or data. It is often used to control source code by programmers who are developing software collaboratively.” [Wikipedia as of 2025-05-30 ]

I. Setup & Configuration

1. Install Git

(Test if installed: git --version)

Windows

winget install --id Git.Git -e --source winget

macOS

brew install git

Linux

# Debian, Ubuntu, Raspberry Pi OS
sudo apt install git

# Arch Linux
sudo pacman -S git

# Fedora
sudo dnf install git

# NixOS / Nix
nix-env -iA nixpkgs.git

2. Install GitHub CLI (gh)

Essential for PRs and repo management from the terminal.
